Description: Identified a pattern that may indicate AWS credentials, risking unauthorized cloud resource access and data breaches on AWS platforms.
Author: mike
Message:
feat(pi): add opt-in sidecar interaction trace

Introduces an append-only sidecar JSONL trace under ~/.pi/agent/traces/<session-id>.jsonl
that captures provider request payloads, tool calls and results, prompt-router
decisions, model selection, and session lifecycle events. Disabled by default;
enable via ~/.pi/agent/settings.json (the repo-tracked pi/settings.json is
intentionally never consulted for the toggle).

- Append-only writer with secret redaction, oversized-field spill to gzip,
  retention sweep, and 64 MiB rotation
- W3C Trace Context propagation via TRACEPARENT for subagent processes so
  child spans correlate to the parent run
- session-hooks initializes the writer on session_start and emits
  session_start / session_shutdown lifecycle events
- prompt-router emits routing_decision events alongside the existing Python
  classifier log so they can be post-hoc joined by prompt_hash
- /transcript-purge slash command and 'just transcript' recipe for the
  minimal opt-in extension stack
- Tests cover redaction, correlation, integration flows, and fixture parity

Description: Uncovered a GitHub Personal Access Token, potentially leading to unauthorized repository access and sensitive content exposure.
Author: mike
Message:
docs(specs): pi-tool-reduction Phase 1 plan + review synthesis

Plan for a tokenjuice-seeded bash output compactor with a two-phase
rollout:
- Phase 1: deterministic rule-based reduction, secret-scrubbed corpus
  logger, per-call Python subprocess hook (shipped in this branch).
- Phase 2 (deferred): offline LLM JSON-rule codegen, rule cache with
  version fingerprinting, drift detection, classifier router, and a
  background updater agent.

Includes review-1/synthesis.md from the adversarial review pass
(13 bugs fixed, 8 hardening items applied) and a Completion Log
recording V3 gate results: 144 Python + 6 TS tests green, ruff
clean, 32.4% bytes saved on live corpus, 0% false-positive rate,
p95 latency 621 ms on Windows (down from 14,908 ms via lazy-load).

Description: Uncovered a GitHub Personal Access Token, potentially leading to unauthorized repository access and sensitive content exposure.
Author: mike
Message:
feat(pi): tool-reduction core Python package

Deterministic bash output compactor modeled on tokenjuice:
- pipeline.py: pure applyRule pipeline (strip_ansi, skipPatterns,
  keepPatterns, dedupeAdjacent, trimEmptyEdges, counters, head_tail,
  onEmpty, preserveOnFailure) with JS-flag translation
- regex_guard.py: ReDoS protection (static reject + SIGALRM/thread timeout)
- rules.py: 3-layer overlay loader (builtin < user < project), argv +
  gitSubcommands matcher, lazy argv0-indexed load on hot path
- guards.py: selectInlineText passthrough guard (TINY_OUTPUT_MAX_CHARS=240)
  and clamp utilities
- scrub.py: 13-kind secret scrubber (github, stripe, aws, google, slack,
  jwt, bearer, ssh, url password, env secrets)
- corpus.py: jsonl logger with portalocker atomic append, daily rotation,
  scrub before sample truncation
- reduce.py: orchestrator CLI (stdin JSON request -> stdout JSON response)
- evaluate.py: eval harness with --min-reduction / --max-fp gates
- build_index.py: generates rules/builtin/_index.json for lazy load

Tests: 144 pytest cases covering all modules + concurrent corpus append.
Docs: labeling-protocol.md, baseline-latency.md (p95 621 ms on Windows
after lazy-load).

Message:
test(hooks): add coverage tests raising hook files from 36-61% to 82-96%

New test files:
- test_edit_write_coverage.py (78 tests): edit/write hooks 36%→82%
- test_injection_coverage.py (33 tests): injection detection 45%→96%
- test_sequence_cli.py (23 tests): sequence-detector CLI 66%→93%
Modified test_session_history.py (+21 tests): session history 61%→89%

Overall hook coverage: 65%→82%

Author: mike
Message:
style(python): format and lint all Python files

Auto-format 60 Python files with ruff:
- Sorted imports (I001)
- Modernized typing annotations (UP006/UP035)
- Fixed line length violations (E501)

Changes are automatic formatter output, zero behavioral changes.
All tests pass (252/252).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: mike
Message:
test: Add comprehensive test suite for damage-control enhancements

Add 156 total test cases across three test layers:

1. Unit Tests (94 tests in test_semantic_analysis.py):
   - 27 shell unwrapping tests (bash/sh/python/env wrappers, nesting, edges)
   - 35 git semantic analysis tests (checkout, push, reset, clean)
   - 28 audit logging tests (log creation, secret redaction, JSONL format)
   - 4 integration tests combining all features

2. Integration Tests (42 tests in test_fixtures.yaml):
   - 10 shell unwrapping test cases
   - 15 git semantic test cases
   - 17 backward compatibility test cases

3. Smoke Tests (18 tests in damage-control.bats):
   - Installation structure validation
   - Hook functionality validation
   - Documentation verification

All 94 pytest tests pass in 0.32s. All 42 integration tests pass (100% rate).
Tests use pytest fixtures for isolated log directory testing and comprehensive
edge case coverage including nested wrappers, combined flags, and secret patterns.
